title: The Stormlight Archive
author: Brandon Sanderson

books.title: Wind and Truth
books.review: An epic end to the first era of SLA.

review: One of the greatest epic fantasy series ever written. Sanderson's world-building is unmatched — Roshar is a fully realized world with its own ecology, magic systems, and history.

title: Mistborn Era 1
author: Brandon Sanderson

books.title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
books.review: An introduction to the world of Scadriel, following a pair of Allomancers who use metals to fuel their magics. This unique system of magic is core to the world and those that can control all of the metals are seen as demi-gods.

books.title: The Well of Ascension
books.review: After the events of 'The Final Empire' Vin and Elend must navigate a difficult political climate. A good follow-up to book 1, though what felt like an un-necessary love triangle forced in.

books.title: The Hero of Ages
books.review: Cataclysm faces Scadriel after the events of 'The Well of Ascension.' Vin, Elend, and Sazed must work to prevent this, while also discovering the secrets of a different metal art. Good ending to Era 1 with consequences to ther greater Cosmere.

review: A gripping heist-fantasy with one of the most unique magic systems in the genre. Allomancy — powered by swallowing and burning metals — makes every fight and scene feel fresh. A must-read Sanderson entry point.

title: Secret Projects
author: Brandon Sanderson

books.title: Yumi and the Nightmare Painter
books.review: The third book in Sanderson's 'Secret Projects' kickstarter. I will admit I had difficulty getting into this one comapred to the earlier two. I will have to give it another listen/read in the future. The story was a bit predictable and I guessed the twist farily early through. I should be used to the back and forth narration style of Michael and Kate by now with WoT and SLA, but for some reason I couldn't quite jive with it this time.

review: A collection of novels that Brandon Sanderson developed as part of a Kickstarter to explore the journies of Hoid and his experiences across the Cosmere. Mostly told with Hoid as a neutral third party.

title: Dungeon Crawler Carl
author: Matt Dinniman

books.title: Dungeon Crawler Carl
books.review: Hilarious, creative, and surprisingly emotional. Carl and Princess Donut are iconic.

books.title: The Gate of the Feral Gods

review: Hilarious, creative, and surprisingly emotional. Carl and Princess Donut are one of the best duos in modern fiction. Gets better with every book — Jeff Hays' narration on the audiobooks is a masterclass.
